sections.problem: A product change (new checkout flow) was run as an A/B test. The team needed a clear answer: did the treatment improve conversion, and by how much?
sections.data: Simulated experiment: 50k users per arm, binary outcome (converted or not). Control conversion ~4.2%, treatment ~4.55%. I checked balance on covariates and looked for early stopping or novelty effects.
sections.approach: I computed the difference in conversion rates, a two-proportion z-test, and a 95% CI for the lift. I also estimated required sample size (power analysis) for a minimum detectable effect. No multiple-comparison correction was needed for this single primary metric.
sections.results: Observed lift +8.2% relative (4.2% → 4.55%), 95% CI [2.1%, 14.5%], p < 0.01. The chart shows conversion by arm with error bars.
next: Segment analysis (e.g. by device or cohort).
Long-term metrics (retention, revenue) once data is available.
